task_completion.description: Navigate to the Segment Statistics module
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Segment Statistics' module is visible in the module panel or selected via the module finder.

task_completion.description: Select the correct Segmentation and Scalar Volume nodes
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Segment Statistics module, the 'Segmentation' dropdown is set to the tumor segmentation node and 'Scalar volume' is set to the MRI volume (not 'None').
The 'Apply' button is clicked or the status indicates calculation is complete.

task_completion.description: Verify the statistics table contains the correct segments and data
task_completion.visual_evidence: A table is visible at the bottom of the module showing rows for 'Necrotic', 'Edema', and 'Enhancing', with columns for Volume and intensity statistics.
The 'Export to file' button (or similar export icon) is clicked.
The file save dialog shows the filename set to 'tumor_statistics.csv'.

task_completion.description: Save the file to the specified directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file save dialog or file explorer shows the path '~/Documents/SlicerData/Exports/' (or 'Documents/SlicerData/Exports') as the destination.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent uses the GUI module, not Python scripting
integrity.visual_evidence: The Python console should not be used to script the 'SegmentStatisticsLogic' directly; the user must interact with the module panel.

integrity.description: Ensure statistics are calculated, not manually typed
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should not be seen typing numbers into a text editor or spreadsheet manually; the data must come from the Slicer export.

integrity.description: Verify interaction with the actual 3D Slicer application
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the standard 3D Slicer GUI layout (3D view, slice views, module panel), not a mock-up or different software.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct segments to look for in the statistics table are 'Necrotic', 'Edema', and 'Enhancing'. The table must include a 'Volume' column (often labeled 'Volume mm3') and intensity columns. The final output file must be named 'tumor_statistics.csv'.
